title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
subTitle: Comedians Jamali Maddix and Larry Dean journey to Bhutan, exploring the mysterious Land of the Thunder Dragon in this remote Himalayan kingdom.

title: Spectacular Earth
subTitle: A group of brave experts containing filmmakers and adventurers, look for the most stunning natural phenomena around the world in order to apprehend the beauty.

title: Deadly 60
subTitle: Steve sets sail to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish. He rescues fish trapped in an abandoned net and meets the teenagers fighting to ban plastic bags on the island.

title: Deadly 60
subTitle: Steve Backshall, along with his crew, travels to different parts of the world in order to find the 60 most deadly animals living on the planet.

title: Mammals
subTitle: The dark is a world of opportunity for mammals. Additionally, more than two-thirds prefer the cover of the night, with some now returning to the safety of the shadows to avoid humans.

subTitle: Comedians Jamali Maddix and Larry Dean journey to Bhutan, exploring the mysterious Land of the Thunder Dragon in this remote Himalayan kingdom.

title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
subTitle: On an autumn in Bordeaux, Rick Stein savours gratton with radishes, rabbit with prunes and a winemaker's barbecued cote de bôeuf with a marrowbone topping.

title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
subTitle: Ade Adepitan embarks on the first leg of his journey around Africa, starting in West Africa from Cape Verde to Senegal and the Ivory Coast, before finishing in Nigeria.

title: Spectacular Earth
subTitle: A group of brave experts containing filmmakers and adventurers, look for the most stunning natural phenomena around the world in order to apprehend the beauty.

title: Deadly 60
subTitle: Steve heads to Norway to dive with one of the planet's ultimate predator, the orca. He also witnesses the northern lights and gets up close to the mighty mouth of a humpback whale.

title: Deadly 60
subTitle: Steve's search for deadly animals takes him and the crew to South Africa. This time they are looking for one of the most iconic of them all, the ferocious great white shark.

title: Mammals
subTitle: For millions of years, mammals have adapted to diverse environments, but rapid changes driven by humans challenge their survival.

title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
subTitle: Comedians Olga Koch and Thanyia Moore push their limits on a thrilling journey across two massive mountain ranges in Bulgaria.

title: Spectacular Earth
subTitle: Explore the science behind the phenomenon of the shimmering Northern Lights, Aurora Borealis, that stretches across thousands of kilometres in the night sky.

title: Planet Earth II
subTitle: Harsh deserts force animals to come up with ingenious ways of coping with hostile conditions, thereby giving rise to some of the most incredible survivors on earth.

title: Planet Earth II
subTitle: Grasslands support a vast variety of wildlife, but to survive there animals must endure the most hostile seasonal changes on the planet.

title: Planet Earth
subTitle: Cities are growing at a faster rate than any other habitat on Earth. They may seem an unlikely place for animals to thrive, but they can be a world of surprising opportunities.

title: Walking with Dinosaurs
subTitle: 66 million years ago, an orphaned baby Triceratops roamed the lush world of Laramidia, alone and vulnerable. Stalked by the fearsome Tyrannosaurus Rex, the youngster relied on her wits.

title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
subTitle: Rick Stein arrives in Vienna in order to try popular dishes like zwiebelrostbraten, tafelspitz, apple strudel and sachertorte.

title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
subTitle: Rick Stein embarks on his adventure in Bologna by tasting Bolognese sauce and sweet onion raviolini. Later, he tries his hand at various local recipies and explores ancient architecture.

title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
subTitle: Rick Stein begings his adventure in Lisbon with a delicious custard tart and moves onto pregos, clams, prawns and carabineros. Later, he cooks an almond tart in his kitchen in Padstow.

title: Chasing Monsters
subTitle: Cyril treks to the Cree First Nations territory in Canada to try to catch a predatory northern pike while battling freezing weather.

subTitle: After their boat was stolen, Cyril Chaquet and his team end up trekking and treading through the dangerous woods and waters of the Amazon.

title: Chasing Monsters
subTitle: Adventurer Cyril Chauquet embarks on a journey to find the most dangerous underwater creatures in the most remote parts of the world.

subTitle: People living in Alaska talk about their daily struggles and explain the different techniques they use to survive in a sub-zero-degree environment.

title: Life Below Zero
subTitle: Sue tries to hunt primitively and Andy takes the new puppies to his sled team. Meanwhile, the Hailstones take baby Wade to snare rabbits.

title: Life Below Zero
subTitle: Sue checks her new property at Chena River, Ricko traps a beaver and Jessie trains puppies for dog sledding. Meanwhile, Agnes, Edward, and Idi gather wood in harsh Alaskan conditions.

title: A Perfect Planet
subTitle: Without volcanoes, there would be no life on Earth. Although destructive, magma from the planet's molten core forms land and mineral-rich ash from eruptions fertilises the surface.

title: China: Nature's Ancient Kingdom
subTitle: Tibetan Qinghai Plateau is home to animals such as snow leopards and foxes who have adapted to survive in the rugged landscape of western China.

title: Blue Planet II
subTitle: The filmmaker gives a retrospective insight into the behavioural patterns and natural habitats of several rare but strange creatures living deep under the sea.

title: Planet Earth II
subTitle: There are numerous remote islands around the world that offer sanctuary for some of the planet's strangest and rarest creatures.

title: Frozen Planet II
subTitle: David Attenborough narrates an icy adventure through the world's frozen zones, where wolves hunt, penguins huddle, and life thrives against the most extreme odds.

title: Wild Scandinavia
subTitle: While the coasts of Scandinavia are wild and unpredictable, it is also home to a wide rage of fauna like sea eagles, otters, base jumpers and orca.

title: Planet Earth III
subTitle: David Attenborough potrays the efforts of numerous conservation advocates who risk everything in order to rescue wildlife.

title: Big Little Journeys
subTitle: The filmmaker captures the journeys of two young creatures as one navigates towering forests in Canada in search of wetlands while the other follows distant lights in South Africa.

title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
subTitle: David Attenborough explores the continent of Antarctica and the challenges faced by its inhabitants due to changing climate.

title: Wild Alaska
subTitle: During springtime in Alaska, wilderness awakens and chaos ensues among various animals as they compete with each other for resources.

title: A Perfect Planet
subTitle: The host discusses various methods in which Earth and its natural resources like volcanoes, water bodies and other environmental phenomenons mould themselves in order to support life.

title: Nature's Greatest Dancers
subTitle: Steve Backshall explores the various animals, like ring-tailed lemurs and a tiny brown banana frog, that use amorous glances and tantalising moves in pursuit of romance.

title: The Hunt
subTitle: The contests between predators and prey are the most dramatic events in nature. Explore the extraordinary range of techniques employed by animals in order to trap their target.

title: Dynasties
subTitle: In Senegal, West Africa, live a group of chimpanzees, led by an alpha male named David. He has already been alpha for 3 years - a time when leaders here are usually overthrown. To make matters worse, David has no allies - no-one to help him.

title: Serengeti
subTitle: There's humour, heartbreak and nail-biting tension as our animal families face new tests and fresh rivalries. Soon a devastating storm creates the biggest problem of all.

title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
subTitle: The presenter explores Australia, a land cast adrift at the time of the dinosaurs. Additionally, the weird and wonderful animals marooned in this place exist like nowhere else on Earth.

subTitle: The filmmaker gives a retrospective insight into the behavioural patterns and natural habitats of several rare but strange creatures living deep under the sea.

subTitle: The host discusses various methods in which Earth and its natural resources like volcanoes, water bodies and other environmental phenomenons mould themselves in order to support life.

subTitle: The filmmaker sheds light on the lives of individual African big cats and explores their behaviour that allows them to thrive in the challenging and breathtaking landscapes of Botswana.

title: Joanna Lumley's Spice Trail Adventure
subTitle: The last leg of Joanna Lumley's journey takes her to Zanzibar, the spice island of Africa where merchants from around the world have left their mark.

title: Inside the Factory
subTitle: Gregg Wallace follows the production of pocket-sprung mattresses, from the arrival of hard steel to soft bedding heading out of dispatch, at a massive factory.

title: Chasing Monsters
subTitle: Cyril Chauquet is assigned the dangerous task of capturing an electric eel that is believed to emit a shock powerful enough to kill a human.

title: Earth's Sacred Wonders
subTitle: At the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem, an orthodox Christian must deliver a holy flame into the hands of his bishop; at the Boudhanath in Nepal, a Buddhist painter battles the elements in honor of Buddha's birthday.
